evals.prompt: Give me a first-pass PowerPoint deck for our Q3 HR update. Agenda: hiring recap, onboarding changes. Hiring recap: 12 new hires, time-to-fill down 18%. Onboarding changes: new day-one packet, manager check-in moved to day 3.
evals.expected_output: Runs skills/octave-docs/scripts/text_to_pptx.py against an outline built from the request and reports the output .pptx path under posts/octave-docs/
expectations: Output path ends in .pptx and lives under posts/octave-docs/
Verified the output opens via python-pptx before reporting success
Reported the output path back to the user

evals.prompt: Convert this into a first-pass Word memo using the Octave template: Title 'Q3 HR Team Update'. Section 'Hiring Recap' with body text about 12 new hires. Section 'Onboarding Changes' with body text about the new day-one packet.
evals.expected_output: Runs skills/octave-docs/scripts/text_to_docx.py against an outline built from the request and reports the output .docx path under posts/octave-docs/
expectations: Output path ends in .docx and lives under posts/octave-docs/
Verified the output opens via python-docx before reporting success
Reported the output path back to the user

evals.prompt: Build a deck: Title 'Team Update'. A section called 'Wins' with no bullet points under it — just the section name as a divider. Then a slide 'Details' with bullets: shipped feature X, fixed bug Y.
evals.expected_output: The bullet-less 'Wins' heading is written as a Section Header slide; 'Details' is written as a Title and Content slide with two bullets
expectations: The outline passed to text_to_pptx.py has 'Wins' as a heading with no bullets under it
Does not force bullets onto 'Wins' or invent placeholder bullet text

evals.prompt: python-pptx is not installed in this environment. The user asks for a first-pass PowerPoint deck.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects the missing dependency via the step-1 import check and stops, instructing the user to run pip3 install python-pptx python-docx, without attempting to generate a fake or partial output
expectations: Ran or referenced the python3 -c "import pptx, docx" dependency check before attempting generation
Did not produce or claim to produce a .pptx output
Told the user the exact install command: pip3 install python-pptx python-docx
Stopped and waited rather than guessing at a workaround
